message:
chore(beads): close bd-1l6x (fsfs/fusion/embed fsync hardening)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -25,6 +25,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1hw","title":"Incremental Append-Only FSVI Index Updates","description":"Implement WAL-based append-only mutation for the FSVI vector index format. Currently, any document addition requires a full index rebuild. This is the #1 usability friction point for frankensearch consumers.

## Background

The FSVI (FrankenSearch Vector Index) format is frankensearch's custom binary vector index. It stores document embeddings in a compact, memory-mappable layout optimized for top-k cosine similarity search. However, the current design is fully immutable: adding a single document requires reading the entire index, appending the new record in memory, and writing a completely new index file. For large indices (e.g., xf with 50K+ tweets), this means re-processing the entire corpus on every addition.

## Design

### Append Buffer (WAL)
New vectors are appended to a separate `.fsvi.wal` file. This file uses the same binary layout as the main index (raw records + vector slabs) but without the header. This keeps the WAL format trivially compatible with the main index reader.

### Search Merges
`top_k_search` reads both the main index and the WAL, merges results via a unified BinaryHeap. The WAL is small relative to the main index, so the merge overhead is minimal.

### Compaction
When the WAL exceeds a configurable threshold (default: 10% of main index size or 1000 records), compact by rewriting main index + WAL into a new main index. This is a background operation that does not block reads.

### Atomic Swap
Compaction writes to `.fsvi.new`, then `rename()` over the old file (atomic on POSIX). This ensures readers never see a partially-written index.

### fsync Discipline
WAL append is fsync'd per batch (not per record) for durability without excessive I/O. Single appends are batches of size 1.

## API Surface

```rust
impl VectorIndex {
    pub fn append(&mut self, doc_id: &str, vector: &[f32]) -> Result<(), SearchError>;
    pub fn append_batch(&mut self, entries: &[(String, Vec<f32>)]) -> Result<(), SearchError>;
    pub fn compact(&mut self) -> Result<CompactionStats, SearchError>;
    pub fn needs_compaction(&self) -> bool;
    pub fn wal_record_count(&self) -> usize;
}
```

## Performance Targets

- Single append: <100us (excluding fsync)
- Batch append (100 docs): <5ms
- Search overhead from WAL merge: <10% vs main-only search (for WAL size < 10% of main)

## Justification

Without incremental append, every consumer must rebuild the entire index when adding a single document. For xf (50K+ tweets), this means re-embedding everything. Append-only makes frankensearch practical for live, growing datasets. This is the single highest-impact usability improvement for all three consumers (cass, xf, mcp_agent_mail_rust).

## Considerations

- WAL file must be crash-safe: partial writes should be detectable (length check + CRC per batch)
- Compaction must not block concurrent reads
- Memory-mapped WAL: for small WALs, mmap is fine; for large WALs approaching compaction threshold, sequential read may be preferred
- Interaction with soft-delete tombstones: WAL entries can also be tombstoned, and compaction naturally removes them

## Testing

- [ ] Unit: append single vector, verify searchable immediately
- [ ] Unit: append batch, verify all vectors searchable
- [ ] Unit: compaction reduces file count, search results unchanged
- [ ] Unit: WAL threshold detection (needs_compaction)
- [ ] Unit: wal_record_count accuracy
- [ ] Integration: concurrent append + search (verify no corruption)
- [ ] Integration: crash recovery (partial WAL write detection)
- [ ] Benchmark: append latency (single and batch)
- [ ] Benchmark: search overhead vs WAL size (1%, 5%, 10%)","acceptance_criteria":"1. The feature is fully implemented according to this bead design, scope, and referenced files or modules.
2. Integration with dependent components is complete and behavior remains correct across normal and degraded flows.
3. Edge and error cases are explicitly handled with deterministic outcomes and clear reason codes or errors.
4. Unit and integration tests (and e2e where relevant) are added, passing, and include detailed structured logging assertions.
5. Performance and resource expectations in the bead are validated and documented with reproducible evidence.
6. Every explicit checklist item in the bead description is implemented and verified by automated tests or validation commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:58:09.662893964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T03:08:40.579699865Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T03:08:40.579613313Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hw","depends_on_id":"bd-3un","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:12:47.455386681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1hw","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:01:54.353544279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1hw","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:01:57.555118045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":302,"issue_id":"bd-1hw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 1: Added cross-dep on bd-sot (soft-delete tombstones). Compaction MUST be tombstone-aware: when rewriting main index + WAL into new main index, tombstoned entries in both WAL and main index must be dropped. This unifies vacuum (bd-sot) and compaction (bd-1hw) into a single operation. Also: WAL append does NOT need asupersync -- it is a synchronous file I/O operation (write + optional fsync). The background compaction thread could use Rayon or a plain std::thread since it is CPU+IO bound, not async IO. If the index refresh worker (bd-3un.28) uses asupersync, then compaction scheduling should integrate with that workers Cx lifecycle.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:02Z"},{"id":313,"issue_id":"bd-1hw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REFINEMENT PASS 1 (correction): Removed hard dep on bd-sot (soft-delete tombstones). Compaction can be implemented without tombstone awareness in the first pass -- it simply merges WAL records into the main index. Tombstone-aware compaction (dropping deleted records during merge) is a SOFT interaction that should be added when bd-sot is implemented. When bd-sot lands, compaction should be updated to skip tombstoned records during the merge pass. This avoids a priority inversion (P1 blocked by P2).","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:08:41Z"},{"id":367,"issue_id":"bd-1hw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"test comment","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:49:51Z"},{"id":368,"issue_id":"bd-1hw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRATE PLACEMENT: frankensearch-index (WAL append, compaction, and merged search are extensions of the FSVI vector index format defined in index/src/format.rs)","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:49:59Z"},{"id":378,"issue_id":"bd-1hw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE FLAGS: No new feature flag needed. Incremental FSVI append is core vector index functionality, always available when frankensearch-index is compiled. The append/compact APIs are unconditional extensions of VectorIndex.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:50:41Z"}]}

Validation via rch local-circuit: cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets --features frankensearch-migration -- -D warnings PASS; cargo +nightly check --all-targets --features frankensearch-migration PASS; rustfmt --check on touched files PASS.","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:23:08Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3un.36.4","title":"Post-cutover cleanup: remove legacy search stack from coding_agent_session_search after frankensearch parity sign-off","description":"Post-cutover decommission plan for `/data/projects/coding_agent_session_search` (cass) after frankensearch migration.

Start gate (all MUST be satisfied before implementation starts):
1) `bd-3un.36` is closed with migration parity/bakeoff evidence.
2) frankensearch-migration CI + local reproduction lanes are green with documented command evidence.
3) Rollback command/path is validated and documented.
4) Migration artifact set (shadow/cutover evidence + replay handles) is complete.

Scope (required):
- Remove dead legacy search modules/helpers superseded by frankensearch-backed search.
- Remove obsolete feature flags, config fields, and env vars tied only to legacy path.
- Remove duplicate legacy-only tests/benchmarks; replace with retained-path assertions where coverage would otherwise drop.
- Update docs/runbooks/operator guidance to single-owner frankensearch path.

Non-goals / guardrails:
- No feature regressions for session-search users.
- No changes to output contracts consumed by automation unless explicitly approved and documented.
- No unrelated cleanup.
- Any file deletion requires explicit written approval from user per AGENTS policy.

Validation matrix (cargo-heavy commands via `rch exec -- ...` in `/data/projects/coding_agent_session_search`):
- Compile: `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets --features frankensearch-migration`
- Lint: `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets --features frankensearch-migration -- -D warnings`
- Unit: retained-path search/index/fusion unit tests covering normal + edge + error flows.
- Integration: migration bakeoff parity lane plus regression suite for session-search scenarios.
- E2E script: create/update `scripts/e2e/post_cutover_cleanup_cass.sh` with representative queries and deterministic assertions.
- Static bug scan: `ubs <changed-files>` with exit code 0.

Logging + artifact bundle required before closure:
- `decommission_manifest.json` (removed/retained surfaces and rationale).
- `decommission_validation_report.md` (commands, outcomes, pass/fail by gate).
- `decommission_replay_command.txt` (deterministic rerun commands).
- e2e structured log pack (`events.jsonl` or equivalent trace logs) including scenario IDs and reason-code outcomes.

Coordination checklist:
- Agent Mail start/completion updates in `br-3un.36.4`.
- Bead comment summarizing removed surfaces, retained contracts, and residual risk (or explicit none).","acceptance_criteria":"1. Start-gate evidence is complete and attached before cleanup implementation begins.
2. Legacy cass search surfaces targeted by this bead are retired without reducing user-visible capabilities or contract stability.
3. Comprehensive unit + integration coverage validates retained frankensearch behavior across happy-path, edge, and failure modes.
4. A deterministic cass e2e cleanup script exists, passes, and emits detailed structured logs for debugging and audit.
5. Required `rch` check/clippy/test lanes and UBS scan pass, with full decommission artifacts linked in bead comments/mail thread.","status":"deferred","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:27:12.239518641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T17:42:46.631602847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cass","cleanup","dead-code","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3un.36.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.36","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:27:12.239518641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1542,"issue_id":"bd-3un.36.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Template reference for this cleanup bead: docs/fsfs-packaging-release-install-contract.md#post-migration-dead-code-decommission-template-required. Use that section for start gates, cleanup surfaces, validation matrix, communication checklist, and artifact bundle requirements.","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:31:21Z"},{"id":1550,"issue_id":"bd-3un.36.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space hardening: cleanup bead moved to deferred and expanded with explicit gates, mandatory unit/integration/e2e validation, detailed structured logging, and reproducible decommission artifacts before closure.","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:42:46Z"},{"id":1583,"issue_id":"bd-3un.36.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Start-gate verification by RedBison:

Gate 1: bd-3un.36 (parent) CLOSED ✅ — Migration complete (assignee: RusticSparrow)
Gate 2: frankensearch-migration feature exists in Cargo.toml ✅ — feature flag at line 117 gates frankensearch-embed and frankensearch-index deps
Gate 3: Rollback = revert cleanup + re-enable feature flag (trivial; the #[cfg] gates are the rollback mechanism)
Gate 4: Migration artifact set: bd-3un.36 closed with parity evidence from RusticSparrow

Investigation summary (2 files affected):
1. src/search/hash_embedder.rs: 11 feature gates. Legacy FNV-1a code (~80 lines) can be removed, making frankensearch delegate the only path.

 {"id":"bd-3un.37","title":"Migrate mcp_agent_mail_rust to use frankensearch crate","description":"Replace mcp_agent_mail_rust search implementation with frankensearch. Agent-mail was the most recent adopter and its search-core crate already has the cleanest separation.

Files to replace in /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust:
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/two_tier.rs → frankensearch::TwoTierSearcher
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/embedder.rs → frankensearch::Embedder
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/model2vec.rs → frankensearch::Model2VecEmbedder
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/fastembed.rs → frankensearch::FastEmbedEmbedder
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/auto_init.rs → frankensearch::EmbedderStack
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/vector_index.rs → frankensearch::VectorIndex
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/fusion.rs → frankensearch::{rrf_fuse, RrfConfig}
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/embedding_jobs.rs → frankensearch background queue

Keep agent-mail-specific:
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/hybrid_candidates.rs (domain-specific budget logic)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/lexical_parser.rs (agent-mail query syntax)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_planner.rs (domain-specific query planning)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/embeddings.rs (SQLite persistence)

The mcp-agent-mail-search-core crate should become a thin wrapper around frankensearch with agent-mail-specific query parsing and persistence.

Migration strategy:
1. Add frankensearch = { path = '../../frankensearch', features = ['hybrid'] }
2. Replace types and implementations incrementally
3. Run search V3 quality gates (SPEC-search-v3-quality-gates.md)
4. Verify TUI search still works with progressive display","acceptance_criteria":"1. The target consumer is migrated to frankensearch integration for the scoped functionality.
2. Behavior and quality parity (or documented improvement) is validated through regression and integration tests on realistic fixtures.
3. Legacy path is removed or explicitly gated with clear fallback semantics and no ambiguous dual ownership.
4. End-to-end workflows are validated with detailed logs and artifacts for triage and rollback decisions.
5. Migration documentation and configuration updates are complete for future operators and maintainers.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticSparrow","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:54:54.462089901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T19:42:06.902409496Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T19:42:06.902387746Z","close_reason":"Completed migration validation evidence; blocker assists closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-mail","migration","phase13"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:55:49.917870995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.40","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:13:47.954171906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","depends_on_id":"bd-ehuk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:24:00.957257244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"AGENT-MAIL MIGRATION NOTES: agent-mail is the most recent adopter and already has the cleanest separation (its search-core crate). The migration should:
1. Replace mcp-agent-mail-search-core internals with frankensearch
2. Keep mcp-agent-mail-db as the persistence layer (SQLite embeddings table)
3. Keep domain-specific query planning in search_planner.rs

agent-mail's feature-gated approach (semantic, hybrid features) maps directly to frankensearch's feature flags. The workspace Cargo.toml can forward features:
  frankensearch = { version = '0.1', features = ['hybrid'] }

The TUI progressive display already consumes SearchPhase — it should work identically with frankensearch's SearchPhase enum.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:57:22Z"},{"id":289,"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW FIX — Quality gate reference, embedding_jobs.rs scope, and async:

1. QUALITY GATE: The migration references "search V3 quality gates (SPEC-search-v3-quality-gates.md)" without inlining the criteria. Key quality gates for the migration:
   - Recall@10 >= 0.85 on the agent-mail ground truth corpus
   - P95 search latency < 200ms for Phase 1 (Initial)
   - P95 search latency < 500ms for Phase 2 (Refined)
   - Zero search errors on the validation suite
   - Progressive display: TUI shows Initial results within 50ms of search start

2. embedding_jobs.rs MIGRATION SCOPE: Not all of embedding_jobs.rs migrates to frankensearch. Keep in agent-mail:
   - Domain-specific batch limits (per mailbox)
   - Priority ordering (new mail > old mail)
   - Mailbox-specific embedding schedules
   Migrate to frankensearch (via bd-3un.27 job queue):
   - Backpressure logic
   - Embedding dispatch
   - Content hash dedup

3. ASYNC COMPATIBILITY: agent-mail already uses an async runtime. The migration to asupersync is straightforward since the TUI progressive display already consumes SearchPhase. Replace the current async runtime integration with asupersync::region().","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:59:53Z"},{"id":1005,"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"[bd-3qwe self-doc] RATIONALE

LOGGING_ARTIFACTS: For bd-3un.37, require structured logs/traces and reproducible artifact references (manifest path, replay command, CI job link).","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:28:28Z"},{"id":1483,"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MIGRATION PHASE 1 COMPLETE — BRIDGE MODULE ESTABLISHED

## What was done

1. **frankensearch dependency wired** into mcp_agent_mail_rust workspace:
   - Added to workspace Cargo.toml: frankensearch, frankensearch-core, frankensearch-embed, frankensearch-index, frankensearch-fusion
   - Added as optional deps in search-core's Cargo.toml (gated behind `semantic` feature)
   - Fixed asupersync path collision (both projects now use relative `../asupersync`)

2. **fs_bridge.rs module created** in mcp-agent-mail-search-core providing:
   - Re-exports of all key frankensearch types with `Fs` prefix to avoid name collisions
   - `doc_id_to_string(u64) -> String` / `doc_id_from_string(&str) -> Option<u64>` for the fundamental doc_id type difference
   - `to_fs_config()` / `from_fs_config()` for TwoTierConfig conversion (f32↔f64 quality_weight)
   - `to_fs_scored_result()` / `from_fs_scored_result()` for ScoredResult conversion
   - `to_fs_model_tier()` / `from_fs_model_tier()` for ModelTier (mapping Hash→Fast)
   - `SyncEmbedderAdapter` wrapping search-core's sync TwoTierEmbedder as frankensearch's async Embedder trait
   - Unit tests for all conversions

3. **Verification**: 1115 tests pass, both search-core and db crates compile with `hybrid` feature

## Key type differences documented

| search-core | frankensearch | Bridge |
|---|---|---|
| `doc_id: u64` | `doc_id: String` | `doc_id_to_string()` / `doc_id_from_string()` |
| `ModelTier::Hash/Fast/Quality` | `ModelTier::Fast/Quality` | Hash→Fast mapping |
| `TwoTierEmbedder` (sync) | `Embedder` (async + `&Cx`) | `SyncEmbedderAdapter` |
| `TwoTierConfig.quality_weight: f32` | `TwoTierConfig.quality_weight: f64` | Widening conversion |
| `ScoredResult` (with DocKind, project_id) | `ScoredResult` (with ScoreSource, metadata) | Domain fields set to defaults |

## Migration phases remaining

**Phase 2**: Replace internal search-core implementations with frankensearch delegates
- model2vec.rs → wrap frankensearch::Model2VecEmbedder + SyncEmbedderAdapter (reverse bridge)
- fastembed.rs → wrap frankensearch::FastEmbedEmbedder + SyncEmbedderAdapter
- Replace SIMD dot product and score blending with frankensearch implementations
- Replace vector_index.rs internal search with frankensearch::VectorIndex

**Phase 3**: Migrate consumers to async Embedder trait
- Change TwoTierEmbedder to async (takes &Cx)
- Update search_service.rs TwoTierBridge to use asupersync region
- Replace HashEmbedder with frankensearch::HashEmbedder

**Phase 4**: Replace orchestration layer
- two_tier.rs → delegate to frankensearch::TwoTierSearcher
- fusion.rs → delegate RRF computation to frankensearch::rrf_fuse
- embedding_jobs.rs → use frankensearch background queue for backpressure

**Phase 5**: Quality gates and cleanup
- Recall@10 >= 0.85 on ground truth corpus
- P95 search latency < 200ms (Initial), < 500ms (Refined)
- TUI progressive display still works with frankensearch SearchPhase
- Remove redundant search-core implementations
","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:20:17Z"},{"id":1486,"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MIGRATION PHASE 2 PARTIAL — EMBEDDER DELEGATION + ERROR MAPPING

## What was done

1. **Error mapping utility** added to `fs_bridge.rs`:
   - `map_fs_error()` maps frankensearch SearchError variants to search-core SearchError
   - ModelNotFound → ModeUnavailable, ModelLoadFailed → Internal, EmbeddingFailed → Internal
   - Cancelled → Timeout, other → Internal(display)
   - Re-exported from lib.rs for use by all modules

2. **model2vec.rs replaced** with thin frankensearch wrapper:
   - Internal struct now wraps `frankensearch::Model2VecEmbedder` (removed tokenizer, embeddings matrix, tensor parsing)
   - `load_from_dir()` delegates to `frankensearch::Model2VecEmbedder::load_with_name()`
   - `try_load()` uses `frankensearch_embed::find_model_dir()` first (HuggingFace, XDG, env var),
     then falls back to agent-mail-specific paths (~/.local/share/mcp-agent-mail/models/, ~/.cache/mcp-agent-mail/models/)
   - `embed()` delegates to `inner.embed_sync()` with error mapping, preserving empty-text error semantics
   - Removed ~180 lines of duplicate implementation code (tensor parsing, l2_normalize, safetensors loading)
   - All constants, global init, and public API preserved

3. **fastembed.rs delegation deferred** to Phase 3:
   - frankensearch uses `asupersync::sync::Mutex` (async) while search-core needs `std::sync::Mutex` (sync)
   - Cannot delegate embed() without async context (&Cx) — requires Phase 3 async migration
   - fastembed.rs left as-is (already simple and correct)

4. **Previously completed in Phase 2** (from Phase 1 session):
   - SIMD dot_product_f16_simd() → frankensearch::index::simd::dot_product_f16_f32()
   - normalize_scores() → frankensearch::fusion::normalize::normalize_scores()
   - EmbedderStack wiring via create_fs_embedder_stack() in auto_init.rs

5. **Verification**: 1102 tests pass in search-core, 22 db unit tests pass

## Phase 2 completion status

| Item | Status |
|------|--------|
| SIMD delegation | DONE |
| Score normalization delegation | DONE |
| EmbedderStack wiring | DONE |
| Error mapping utility | DONE |
| model2vec.rs → frankensearch delegate | DONE |
| fastembed.rs → frankensearch delegate | DEFERRED (Phase 3 - needs async) |
| vector_index.rs → frankensearch VectorIndex | DEFERRED (Phase 4 - types too different) |

## Next: Phase 3 (async migration)

- Change TwoTierEmbedder to async (takes &Cx)
- Replace fastembed.rs with frankensearch::FastEmbedEmbedder (async embed)
- Update search_service.rs TwoTierBridge to use asupersync region
- Replace HashEmbedder with frankensearch::HashEmbedder
","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:48:37Z"},{"id":1488,"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session 3 progress (2026-02-15, SunnyAnchor):

Current module is mutable in-memory with upsert/remove + metadata filters (project/doc_kind/model/exclusions) on search. frankensearch::VectorIndex in current API is FSVI file-backed/open-create oriented with string doc IDs and no equivalent filter hooks for this call path. A direct swap would break updater semantics and scoped filtering guarantees.\n2) two_tier.rs: blocked for full delegation to frankensearch::TwoTierSearcher due contract mismatch: current API is sync iterator + sync embedder trait + domain result fields (u64/doc_kind/project_id). frankensearch searcher is async (&Cx) callback model with async Embedder trait and different result envelope/types. Direct replacement is a wider API migration, not a small safe slice.\n\nSmallest safe next delegation points I see:\n- For vector_index: introduce an optional adapter path for unfiltered search-only cases first (keep existing filtered mutable path as canonical).\n- For two_tier: add an opt-in parallel method (e.g., search_via_frankensearch) bridged via fs_bridge conversions + SyncEmbedderAdapter, then compare outputs before switching default path.","created_at":"2026-02-15T16:47:47Z"},{"id":1516,"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Assist probe complete (BronzeElm): investigated mcp-agent-mail-search-core delegation seams for src/vector_index.rs and src/two_tier.rs. Both seams are feasible but blocked from tiny direct swap by contract mismatch. vector_index blocker: metadata-rich filtering + in-place upsert semantics + deterministic (doc_id, doc_kind) map not 1:1 with frankensearch doc-id-string hit API; minimal slice is backend substitution behind existing VectorIndex API with metadata side-map + filter adapter + hit conversion. two_tier blocker: local synchronous phase iterator and explain contract (phase_timings/stage ordering) differ from frankensearch async phase model; minimal slice is phase adapter wrapper converting FsSearchPhase->local SearchPhase while preserving explain inputs. Repro commands (all RCH): check -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core --all-targets --features hybrid PASS; test pattern vector_index PASS (48); test pattern two_tier PASS (54); clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core --all-targets --features hybrid -- -D warnings PASS.","created_at":"2026-02-15T16:51:56Z"},{"id":1519,"issue_id":"bd-3un.37","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Assist evidence from bd-3un.37.1: deeper hybrid/Search-V3 validation in /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust is green via rch local-circuit fallback (remote worker lane stalled).
